summary: A snapshot of Andrej Karpathy's X (Twitter) profile containing his thoughts on the evolution of programming and human skills in the age of AI. He highlights the shift toward natural language as a programming interface, introduces the concept of 'vibe coding', and argues that human agency is now far more valuable than raw intelligence.
themes: Human skills in the AI era

principles.statement: Agency is significantly more powerful and scarce than intelligence.
principles.rationale: Because intelligence is becoming commoditized and readily available via AI, the ability to take action and drive outcomes (agency) is now the true differentiator, despite society's historical obsession with IQ.
principles.source_quote: Agency > Intelligence I had this intuitively wrong for decades, I think due to a pervasive cultural veneration of intelligence, various entertainment/media, obsession with IQ etc. Agency is significantly more powerful and significantly more scarce.

principles.statement: English is the new programming language.
principles.rationale: As Large Language Models become highly capable, natural language is replacing traditional syntax as the primary way to instruct computers.
principles.source_quote: The hottest new programming language is English

frameworks.name: Vibe Coding
frameworks.when_to_apply: When building software using highly advanced LLM-based coding assistants.
steps: Fully give in to the vibes.
Forget that the underlying code even exists.
Talk directly to the AI composer (e.g., using voice-to-text like SuperWhisper).
frameworks.source_quote: There's a new kind of coding I call "vibe coding", where you fully give in to the vibes, embrace exponentials, and forget that the code even exists.

mental_models.name: Vibe Coding
mental_models.description: Treating software development not as writing syntax, but as guiding an AI through natural language and intuition, abstracting away the underlying code entirely.
mental_models.example: Using Cursor Composer with Claude Sonnet and talking to it via SuperWhisper to build applications without looking at the code.

signature_quotes.text: The hottest new programming language is English
signature_quotes.context: Commenting on the shift towards natural language prompting for AI.

signature_quotes.text: Agency > Intelligence I had this intuitively wrong for decades, I think due to a pervasive cultural veneration of intelligence, various entertainment/media, obsession with IQ etc.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on what human traits are most valuable now that AI provides intelligence on demand.

signature_quotes.text: There's a new kind of coding I call "vibe coding", where you fully give in to the vibes, embrace exponentials, and forget that the code even exists.
signature_quotes.context: Describing the experience of programming with advanced LLMs like Cursor Composer.

anti_patterns.description: Venerating intelligence and obsessing over IQ when evaluating or hiring people.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Intelligence is becoming widely available ('on tap') via AI, making it less of a differentiator. Focusing on intelligence causes you to miss out on agency, which is much more powerful and scarce.
